meta.portfolioName: GoWell Energy Solutions — Zhang Family Trust Real Estate Portfolio
meta.preparedBy: Atlas Point by BPM — Asset Intelligence Platform

summary.narrative: The Zhang Family Trust real estate portfolio comprises nine assets across five jurisdictions — Singapore, the United States (Houston, TX), Malaysia (Johor), Hong Kong SAR, and the United Arab Emirates (Dubai). As at May 2026, the portfolio's aggregate assessed value stands at USD 31.27 million, with total senior debt of USD 7.01 million, resulting in a blended LTV of 22.4%. The portfolio is structured around three strategic pillars: (i) corporate infrastructure supporting GoWell Energy Solutions' operations (GoWell Singapore HQ, GoWell US Houston Division dormitory complex), (ii) long-horizon greenfield capital deployment via the Pengerang industrial land in Johor, targeted as the future GoWell OCTG Logistics Hub, and (iii) international wealth preservation and lifestyle assets across Hong Kong, Dubai, and Singapore. Income-generating assets yield aggregate gross annual rental income of approximately USD 183,000. In preparation for GoWell's planned SGX Mainboard IPO (target H1 2027), corporate-held assets are ring-fenced from Zhang family personal holdings within the trust structure. The most time-critical action item is the Houston dormitory complex facility refinancing, with the existing Regions Bank term loan maturing in August 2026 — lender outreach should commence by June 2026 at the latest.
summary.keyMessage: Portfolio AUM USD 31.3M · Zhang Family Net Equity USD 22.4M · Blended LTV 22.4% · CRITICAL: Houston facility refinancing due August 2026 — engage lenders by June 2026

riskSummary.topConcern: GoWell US Inc. Regions Bank term loan (USD 4.1M, secured on Houston dormitory complex) matures 15 August 2026. With US commercial real estate lending conditions tightened, and the property's subsidised-use profile reducing appraised income, refinancing must be initiated no later than June 2026. Failure to refinance in time risks a default event which could trigger cross-default clauses in GoWell's corporate borrowing facilities and materially jeopardise the SGX IPO timeline.
